nodes.label: Marie Curie
nodes.description: Polish-born physicist and chemist, first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
source_sentences: Marie Curie was a Polish-born physicist and chemist who became the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
Born Maria Skłodowska in Warsaw in 1867, she moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne.

nodes.label: Warsaw
nodes.description: Polish city where Marie Curie was born in 1867.
source_sentences: Born Maria Skłodowska in Warsaw in 1867, she moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne.

nodes.label: Paris
nodes.description: French city where Marie Curie studied and conducted her research.
source_sentences: Born Maria Skłodowska in Warsaw in 1867, she moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne.

nodes.label: Sorbonne
nodes.description: Parisian university where Marie Curie pursued her studies.
source_sentences: Born Maria Skłodowska in Warsaw in 1867, she moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne.

nodes.label: Pierre Curie
nodes.description: French physicist, husband and research partner of Marie Curie.
source_sentences: She married Pierre Curie in 1895, and together they discovered the elements polonium and radium.

nodes.label: polonium
nodes.description: Chemical element discovered by Marie and Pierre Curie.
source_sentences: She married Pierre Curie in 1895, and together they discovered the elements polonium and radium.

nodes.label: radium
nodes.description: Chemical element discovered by Marie and Pierre Curie; subject of her second Nobel Prize.
source_sentences: She married Pierre Curie in 1895, and together they discovered the elements polonium and radium.
Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, shared with Pierre and Henri Becquerel, and a second Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911 for her work on radium.

nodes.label: Henri Becquerel
nodes.description: French physicist who shared the 1903 Nobel Prize with the Curies.
source_sentences: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, shared with Pierre and Henri Becquerel, and a second Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911 for her work on radium.

nodes.label: Nobel Prize in Physics (1903)
nodes.description: Awarded to Marie Curie, Pierre Curie, and Henri Becquerel.
source_sentences: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, shared with Pierre and Henri Becquerel, and a second Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911 for her work on radium.

nodes.label: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1911)
nodes.description: Awarded to Marie Curie for her work on radium.
source_sentences: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, shared with Pierre and Henri Becquerel, and a second Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911 for her work on radium.

nodes.label: Curie Institute
nodes.description: Medical research institution founded by Marie Curie in Paris.
source_sentences: She founded the Curie Institute in Paris, which remains a major center for medical research.
